task_updates.executor_notes: Migrated the remaining stale payment-test fakes to the actor-based `confirm_payment(payment_id, *, actor, guild_id=None)` signature, removed leftover test-only import drift, and verified that the inspected payment-related test modules no longer depend on `PaymentCog` lookups or pre-actor confirmation kwargs. `tests/conftest.py` and `tests/test_caller_paths.py` were inspected during the migration pass and required no edits because they contain no old payment confirmation API or `PaymentCog` references. Verified with a payment-test grep, `python -m py_compile` across the inspected modules, `python -m pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py tests/test_scheduler.py tests/test_social_publish_service.py tests/test_caller_paths.py -q`, and a full `pytest --tb=no -q --no-header` run (`79 passed, 1 warning`).
commands_run: python -m py_compile tests/test_admin_payments.py tests/test_scheduler.py tests/test_social_publish_service.py tests/test_caller_paths.py tests/conftest.py
python -m pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py tests/test_scheduler.py tests/test_social_publish_service.py tests/test_caller_paths.py -q

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed the remaining payment-related test fakes now use the actor-based `confirm_payment` signature, no tests still look up `PaymentCog`, and the existing user-visible assertions remained unchanged while the impacted modules and full suite both pass.
